rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains at least one direct internal contradiction: Section 3 correctly states high-difficulty queries escalate to Tier-2, but the cost paragraph inverts this, writing that the 70B model is reserved for queries the classifier 'marks as easy.' Logical consistency across sections is a primary target of any correctness check.
rationale.R1.3: Table 2 reports p99 tail latency rising from 412 ms (baseline) to 587 ms (FROST), a 42% increase, yet the text characterises this as 'a substantial improvement in tail responsiveness.' The Pareto-improvement claim and the closing deployment recommendation both rest on this misread. Verifying that claims are supported by the reported data is therefore load-bearing.

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'Check this' be interpreted as (A) verifying technical correctness and internal consistency — logical contradictions, data–claim mismatches — or (B) reviewing and improving prose quality and writing style?
